# Zanna Functional Runtime Reference

[Back to the runtime reference index](README.md)

## Classes

<a id="zanna-functional-lazy"></a>
### `Zanna.Functional.Lazy`

Provides Lazy functionality for functional programming workflows.

`Zanna.Functional.Lazy` exposes a registry-backed runtime surface without requiring callers to
construct the class directly. Its public surface exposes properties such as `IsEvaluated` and
operations including `New`, `Of`, `OfStr`, `OfI64`, `Get`. `New` wraps a zero-argument
supplier that is invoked once on first access; the `Of*` factories wrap eager values.

Constructor: `Zanna.Functional.Lazy.New`

#### Properties

| Property | Type | Access |
|---|---|---|
| <a id="zanna-functional-lazy-isevaluated"></a>`IsEvaluated` | `i1` | read-only |

<a id="zanna-functional-lazyseq"></a>
### `Zanna.Functional.LazySeq`

Provides Lazy Seq functionality for functional programming workflows.

`Zanna.Functional.LazySeq` exposes a registry-backed runtime surface without requiring callers
to construct the class directly. Its public surface exposes properties such as `Index`,
`IsExhausted` and operations including `Range`, `Repeat`, `Next`, `Peek`.

#### Properties

| Property | Type | Access |
|---|---|---|

| <a id="zanna-functional-lazyseq-isexhausted"></a>`IsExhausted` | `i1` | read-only |
